mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (Rank IC < 0.02) across a 5-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.5pp monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital investment demand, driving broad-based earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM movements in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns (out-of-sample R-squared < 0.01) or show negative predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects capacity constraints and rising input costs, compressing corporate profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the predictive coefficient flips sign.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.4% signals persistent price pressures that force equity valuations lower, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL prints above 0.4% MoM are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting wage growth and consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to predict positive 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.03) or shows inconsistent directional alignment.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue expansion, directly translating to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth shows no significant positive correlation with 20-day forward returns (t-stat < 1.5) across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production decline exceeding 0.5% month-over-month indicates severe manufacturing weakness that immediately pressures equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines below -0.5% MoM are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances.
